page: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V3 model of the ARMv9 family, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9g.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and top-tier memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yields a strong score of 96,265.95, while single-core performance sits in the average tier at 2,009.19. Memory subsystem benchmarks show top-tier results, with a cached small-block read bandwidth of 6,846.75 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score of 4,719.67. Large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are strong at 187.91 GB/sec and 85.62 GB/sec, respectively. The server also achieves top-tier performance in data decompression, with a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 624.06 GB/sec, and in database operations, processing 136.27 million Redis SET operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARMv9 architecture delivers high resource density and strong multi-threaded performance per unit of hardware resource, though single-core execution speeds remain average. The absence of local storage means all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given its balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, high database operation benchmarks, and top-tier static web serving throughput of 97.07 GB/sec, the m9g.12xlarge is suited for database hosting, web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor on the arm64 architecture. It is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 25 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, highlighted by a PassMark memory score of 4,719.67 and 136.27 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance is average, its high multi-threaded decompression and database processing capabilities make it highly efficient for its resource class. This server is suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 instance with 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 instance with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network speed.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose platform delivering dedicated physical cores, top-tier memory bandwidth, and strong multi-threaded database performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core allocation
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance, including 136.27 million Redis SET operations per second
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads
